sections.title: Before completing Sins of the Father

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you don't look happy.

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: You're not wrong about that. It's my travelling companion, Hanchen. They say he's the latest victim of the Sleeper Plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sleeper Plague?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: Some mysterious illness. You'll have to ask the sisters if you want to know more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look like locals.

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: No, not at all. We came here from afar with the tattooed guy outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How far?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: We travelled eastwards over many oceans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why travel all this way?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: I ask myself the same question. We came here on a quest. You see, I found a poem in the College of Bards that told of the sanctuary below. It seemed worthy of exploration.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: College of Bards? Can't say I've heard of it.

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: No? Have you not visited the grand Kingdom of Varlamore? The college is but one of many attractions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really? Perhaps I should go there sometime.

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: You won't regret it! With all that's happened, I'd much rather be back there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the sanctuary.

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: To be honest, the poem didn't say much. Although I found this while searching this church.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting, I'll give it a read!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: Farewell, traveller.

sections.title: After completing Sins of the Father

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look happy?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: Well it's not like I have much to be happy about. First one of my travelling companions catches the Sleeper Plague, then the other goes and gets executed. We should never have come here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look like locals.

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: No, not at all. We came here from afar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How far?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: We travelled eastwards over many oceans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why travel all this way?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: I ask myself the same question. We came here on a quest. You see, I found a poem in the College of Bards that told of the sanctuary below.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the sanctuary.

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: Everything I know is in that parchment I gave you earlier. You should give it a read.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: Timoiva, traveller.
